The Engineer

Muhammad Bayoumi

Senior Technical Office Engineer & Creator of mbiX

A civil engineer with 4+ years of experience in technical offices, quantity surveying, and cost estimation across large-scale construction projects in Egypt and Saudi Arabia. Frustrated by the daily pain of syncing Google Sheets data into Excel manually, he built mbiX from scratch — a full VSTO add-in with an ETL pipeline, schema governance, and zero external dependencies. What started as a personal productivity tool became a complete Excel Intelligence Platform.

IPMA Level D — Certified Project Manager Saudi Council of Engineers Egyptian Engineering Syndicate
Engineering
Cost Estimation BOQ / Quantity Surveying Tendering Technical Office Project Management Contract Administration
Software
C# / .NET VSTO Excel Interop SQLite ETL Pipelines Google Sheets API COM Interop HTML / CSS / JS
GitHub LinkedIn Email
The Story

Why mbiX?

In every construction project, the same problem repeats: pricing data, material rates, and BOQ breakdowns live in Google Sheets — shared across teams, updated by dozens of people. But the real engineering work happens in Excel. Copy-pasting is error-prone, manual syncing wastes hours every week, and there is no validation layer in between.

mbiX was born from this daily frustration. Instead of waiting for an IT department to build a cloud solution, Muhammad built the tool where the work actually happens — directly inside Excel, with a full ETL pipeline, schema governance, and zero external dependencies. What started as a personal automation script grew into a complete Excel Intelligence Platform.

2021
Joined Samco as Site Engineer, then moved to Technical Office — first exposure to the data sync problem
2023
First mbiX prototype — basic sync from Google Sheets to Excel using C# and VSTO
2024
Full ETL pipeline with validation, schema rules, and PipelineLogger. Promoted to Senior QS and Cost Estimation Engineer
2025
Moved to SAPAC in Riyadh as Senior Technical Office Engineer. Dynamic Ribbon, RSA licensing, Export Engine, and public release of mbiX
